captions.formal: A technician assembles a mechanical component at a workbench, methodically fastening parts in sequence.
captions.sarcastic: Another human heroically tightens a screw, single-handedly justifying the heat death of the universe.
captions.humorous_tech: Watching this assembly is like debugging legacy code: everything's connected, nothing's documented, and one wrong move drops prod.
captions.humorous_non_tech: He's building it with the quiet confidence of a man who definitely did not read the instructions.

captions.formal: A wide aerial shot pans across a coastal landscape as waves meet the shoreline under clear daylight.
captions.sarcastic: Ah yes, the ocean, still doing the exact same thing it has done for four billion years. Riveting stuff.
captions.humorous_tech: The tide comes in, the tide goes out: nature's original retry loop with no exponential backoff.
captions.humorous_non_tech: The beach: nature's way of charging you three hours of traffic for a bit of sand in your sandwich.

captions.formal: Two individuals engage in conversation in an indoor setting, gesturing intermittently while seated.
captions.sarcastic: Two people talk. Groundbreaking cinema. Someone alert the awards committee immediately.
captions.humorous_tech: This conversation is a git merge conflict in real time: both parties committed to the same idea and neither will rebase.
captions.humorous_non_tech: Two friends catching up, each waiting politely for their turn to not listen to the other.
